macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Stock Indexes Rally on Middle East De-escalation Hopes
items.summary: The S&P 500 rose 0.59% to 6,594.90, Dow Jones gained 0.66% to 46,428.57, and Nasdaq advanced 0.77% to 21,929.83 amid perceptions of Trump seeking an off-ramp to the Iran war, alleviating oil price and borrowing cost concerns. Intraday highs saw Dow up 1.20% to 46,677.80, S&P 500 up 1.08% to 6,626.90, and Nasdaq up 1.15% to 22,011.37.[1][2]

items.title: S&P 500 Holds Key Support Amid Relief Rally
items.summary: S&P 500 closed higher by 0.5%, maintaining support between 6500-6575 and 6500-6600 band, with upper trend channel limit at 6700 under watch during seasonal strength period.[1]

items.title: Iran De-escalation Efforts Boost Markets
items.summary: Prospects of US sending 15-point peace plan to Iran and potential talks in Pakistan or Turkey fueled Wall Street gains, easing fears of prolonged energy disruptions.[2]

items.title: Rising Oil Prices Positive for Transportation Stocks
items.summary: Traditional positive correlation noted between rising oil prices and transportation stocks amid oil market stabilizing post-spike.[1]

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Holds Steady at $70,692 Amid Geopolitical Tensions and Fed Policy
items.summary: Bitcoin is trading at $70,692 with a +0.33% 24-hour change following a volatile week influenced by President Trump's five-day postponement of strikes on Iranian power plants and the Fed's hawkish stance. Ethereum sits at $2,146 (-0.18%), while RWA tokens and AI agents see strong gains.

items.title: Crypto Market Matures with Focus on Institutional Demand and Regulatory Expectations
items.summary: The cryptocurrency market is evolving beyond simple Bitcoin-led rallies, with investors assessing institutional demand, U.S. regulatory progress, and macro factors; Bitcoin acts as a proxy for ETF flows and risk appetite.

items.title: Ripple Expands in Asia with RLUSD Stablecoin Pilot for Faster Settlements
items.summary: Ripple is testing its RLUSD stablecoin in Singapore's BLOOM sandbox with Unloq to automate trade finance payments on the XRP Ledger, aiming for near-instant settlements replacing slow manual processes.

items.title: XRP ETFs See Minimal Inflows Amid Weak Institutional Demand
items.summary: Spot XRP ETFs recorded $636K in inflows last week, their first positive week, but this is dwarfed by $31M in March outflows overall, signaling inconsistent institutional interest.

items.title: Bitcoin Price at $71,299 on March 25, Down from Yearly Highs
items.summary: Bitcoin reached $71,299.68 at 9 a.m. ET on March 25, up slightly daily but $16,100 below last year's level, influenced by speculation, adoption, economy, and regulations.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: MIT Develops AI System for Smoother Warehouse Robot Traffic
items.summary: MIT researchers created a hybrid AI system using deep reinforcement learning that boosts warehouse robot throughput by 25% by prioritizing robots to avoid congestion. The adaptable system outperforms traditional methods in simulations mimicking real e-commerce layouts.

items.title: Los Alamos Lab Trains AI Model for Electroplating Optimization
items.summary: Los Alamos National Laboratory successfully trained a generative diffusion-based AI model on electroplating rhenium samples, accurately predicting crack formation and surface roughness to accelerate materials science development. The model reduces the need for extensive physical experiments.

items.title: UF Hosts AI Literacy Conference with SharkAI Project Showcase
items.summary: University of Florida is hosting a two-day conference on March 26-27 for National AI Literacy Day, featuring the SharkAI project that teaches K-12 students machine learning using shark teeth fossils and 3D prints. The curriculum covers AI ethics, bias, and real scientific practices.

items.title: ITU Announces AI for Good Global Summit in Geneva July 2026
items.summary: The ITU will host its seventh AI for Good Global Summit from July 7-10, 2026, in Geneva, focusing on AI governance, standards, agentic AI security, quantum tech, and innovation challenges. The event follows accelerated global AI strategies by governments.

items.title: Agentic AI Tools Market Projected to Reach $94.90 Billion by 2026
items.summary: The agentic AI tools market, driven by LLMs (41% share), reinforcement learning, and multi-agent systems, is forecasted to hit $94.90 billion in 2026, enabling autonomous enterprise ecosystems. It represents 36% of the AI services market by 2030.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Screamer 2026 Released: 90s Racing Legend Returns with Anime-Style Gameplay
items.summary: Milestone has released Screamer 2026, a reimagining of the classic 1990s racing series featuring vibrant anime-style graphics, dual-stick mechanics, and both single-player and multiplayer modes including split-screen action.

items.title: Console Archives TERRA CRESTA Now Available on Nintendo Switch 2 and PlayStation 5
items.summary: HAMSTER Corporation has released Console Archives TERRA CRESTA, a classic 1986 shooting game, on Nintendo Switch 2 and PlayStation 5 for $7.99 with support for 1-2 players and customizable formation designs.

items.title: Mega Man and Neopets Collections Release on Steam
items.summary: Seven remastered Mega Man games with new visuals and online play, along with Neopets: Mega Mini Games Collection, have been released on Steam as part of the week's gaming lineup.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: NAND Leaders Kioxia, SanDisk, and SK hynix Back Nanya Tech's $2.5B Private Placement
items.summary: Major NAND flash memory companies Kioxia, SanDisk, and SK hynix are investing in Taiwan's DRAM maker Nanya Technology through a $2.5 billion private placement to bolster semiconductor production capacity.

items.title: Big Tech Pushes to Fix U.S. Power Grid Amid Data Center Boom
items.summary: Technology and energy companies at CERAWeek conference advocate for grid improvements over off-grid solutions for data centers, as up to 30% of planned U.S. capacity pairs with behind-the-meter power like mobile gas generators.

items.title: Trump Forms Big Tech Advisory Panel on Science and Technology
items.summary: President Trump announces a 13-member President's Council of Advisors on Science and Technology (PCAST) co-chaired by David Sacks and Michael Kratsios, including leaders like Sergey Brin, Mark Zuckerberg, Larry Ellison, and Jensen Huang.
